# The Fog Break

> Peer-reviewed golf physics, written in plain English, with explicit citations.
> We translate the research on equipment and technique into honest, forensic
> explanations — what the gear actually does at impact, what it doesn't, and
> where the marketing stops matching the math.

The Fog Break is a golf publication for players who want to understand the
science behind their equipment and their swing. Every article is grounded in
peer-reviewed mechanics, worked through first principles, and paired with
interactive tools that let readers explore the same physics themselves.

Citations are explicit and linked. When a claim has a number on it, the paper
it came from is named. When a claim is our own synthesis, we say so.

## Published articles

- [What Your Putter Actually Does and Does Not Do at Impact](https://thefogbreak.com/articles/putter-physics.html): A forensic look at blade, mallet, and zero-torque putters through oblique-impact mechanics. Why face angle dominates direction (83–92% of start line), what MOI really buys you, the horizontal gear effect (Lambeth 2020), and why the dimpled ball puts a ceiling on all of it. 18-minute read, 5 figures.

## Interactive tools

- [Putter Face Angle Visualizer](https://thefogbreak.com/tools/putter-face-angle.html): Adjust face angle, putt distance, putter type, and strike location. The tool returns the predicted miss, accounting for face-angle contribution, horizontal gear effect, and impact-ratio roll-out behavior. Built from the same equations used in the putter-physics article.

## Index

- [Homepage](https://thefogbreak.com/): Latest issue and archive.
- [Tools index](https://thefogbreak.com/tools/): All interactive physics tools.

## Editorial standards

- Every physical claim is sourced. We link to the paper or we don't make the
  claim.
- We favor mechanics over brand names. Where we name a model, we name it
  because the design illustrates a principle, not because we endorse it.
- We publish the equations. If the math doesn't support the story, the story
  changes.

## Contact

The Fog Break is an independent publication. For corrections, citations, or
physics questions, the site is at https://thefogbreak.com/.
